title: rubus apiculatus var. abruptorum
intro: Rubus apiculatus var. abruptorum, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a type of wild raspberry native to the Himalayan region. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, dysentery, and fever, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Rubus apiculatus var. abruptorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts to harness its medicinal benefits.
